mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate above 4.25% signals monetary tightening that raises discount rates on equity cash flows and increases borrowing costs for corporations. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as market reprices equity valuations downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.25%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in nonfarm payroll employment signals labor market deterioration and rising recession risk. This triggers equity risk premium expansion and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reduce equity exposure in anticipation of earnings contraction.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market softening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or correlation between PAYEMS momentum and 5-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline below 1200 thousand units signals demand destruction in the residential construction sector and broader consumer confidence erosion. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations and financial sector credit risk.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1229.0 thousand units as of 2007-10-15
regime_hint: housing market downturn with credit stress
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly lower when HOUST < 1200 versus >= 1200 (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index declines month-over-month while unemployment rate rises simultaneously, indicating synchronized supply-side contraction and labor market weakness. This joint deterioration signals recession onset and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as equity risk premium expands.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 0.3% month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp month-over-month
regime_hint: early recession with synchronized demand and supply contraction
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 50% of instances when both triggers are active, or the interaction effect (joint signal) does not explain significantly more variance than either signal alone (F-test p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales decline month-over-month while housing starts fall simultaneously, indicating synchronized consumer demand destruction across housing and consumption. This dual demand shock produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as earnings expectations contract across consumer-facing and construction-linked sectors.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 0.4% month-over-month AND HOUST declines > 2% month-over-month
regime_hint: demand-driven recession with consumer and housing collapse
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when both conditions are met, or Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation measured by CPI remains above 2.5% while federal funds rate is held above 4.0%, indicating the Fed maintains restrictive policy to combat inflation. This sustained real rate tightening produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as real discount rates remain elevated and growth expectations decline.
regime_hint: stagflation risk with inflation persistence and monetary tightening
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly lower when both conditions hold versus when either is absent (t-test p > 0.12), or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.20).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and increased recession probability. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices earnings growth expectations downward and increases equity risk premium in anticipation of demand destruction.
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market softening transitioning to recession
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly lower when UNRATE > 4.5% versus <= 4.5% (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index declines month-over-month while real retail sales decline simultaneously, indicating synchronized contraction in both supply capacity and demand. This dual shock produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices recession probability and equity risk premium expands sharply.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 0.2% month-over-month AND RSAFS declines > 0.3% month-over-month
regime_hint: synchronized supply-demand contraction with recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances when both triggers activate, or the interaction effect does not explain significantly more variance than either signal alone (F-test p > 0.20).
